Data Science II Midterm Project

Midterm Project Group 29

2026-03-24

Preparation and Inspection

Part 1: PFS model (1990 - 1995)

○ Data Partition

Data Visualization and featurePlot

While looking at the features plots, we can observe that:

lymph_nodes: negative relationship with log_PFS — more positive lymph nodes, shorter PFS. The trend appears approximately linear.

Ki67: negative relationship with log_PFS. Higher proliferation index associated with shorter PFS, though the trend is relatively mild.

age: very weak relationship. Smooth curve is flat.

bmi: slight negative relationship.

tumor_size: ngeative relationship. larger tumor size associated with shorter PFS.

##                     [,1]
## age         -0.052177434
## bmi          0.009478872
## tumor_size  -0.301980410
## lymph_nodes -0.316423346
## Ki67        -0.270877871

Notice that:

stage: Stage 1 patients having the highest log_PFS and Stage 3 the lowest (F = 81.0, p < 0.001), more advanced disease stage is associated with shorter PFS.

tumor_grade: Grade 1 patients had notably higher log_PFS, poorer tumor differentiation is associated with worse prognosis.

ER: ER-positive patients had substantially higher log_PFS than ER-negative patients (F = 138.4, p < 0.001)

hormonal_therapy: Patients receiving hormonal therapy had markedly higher log_PFS (F = 89.6, p < 0.001)

ANOVA

##           Variable F_value P_value
## 1            stage  80.999  0.0000
## 2      tumor_grade  50.177  0.0000
## 3               ER 138.382  0.0000
## 4               PR  32.113  0.0000
## 5             HER2  44.093  0.0000
## 6        menopause   0.191  0.6624
## 7          surgery  22.783  0.0000
## 8            chemo  10.346  0.0013
## 9 hormonal_therapy  89.574  0.0000

Model selection based on the resampling results

If we choose to use GAM as the final model:

## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## .outcome ~ menopause1 + race2 + race3 + race4 + tumor_grade2 + 
##     tumor_grade3 + ER1 + PR1 + HER21 + stage2 + stage3 + surgery1 + 
##     chemo1 + hormonal_therapy1 + s(lymph_nodes) + s(tumor_size) + 
##     s(bmi) + s(Ki67) + s(age)
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)        2.064124   0.085336  24.188  < 2e-16 ***
## menopause1         0.060813   0.051741   1.175  0.24004    
## race2              0.078155   0.049164   1.590  0.11210    
## race3              0.092519   0.062178   1.488  0.13696    
## race4             -0.042246   0.052874  -0.799  0.42441    
## tumor_grade2      -0.072889   0.051044  -1.428  0.15350    
## tumor_grade3      -0.176677   0.065561  -2.695  0.00712 ** 
## ER1                0.242558   0.057105   4.248 2.29e-05 ***
## PR1               -0.028336   0.042651  -0.664  0.50655    
## HER21             -0.205080   0.042287  -4.850 1.36e-06 ***
## stage2             0.036962   0.057158   0.647  0.51795    
## stage3             0.090455   0.112130   0.807  0.41996    
## surgery1          -0.039580   0.036655  -1.080  0.28040    
## chemo1             0.004067   0.035795   0.114  0.90955    
## hormonal_therapy1  0.128418   0.043718   2.937  0.00336 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                  edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## s(lymph_nodes) 1.000  1.000 27.684 4.74e-07 ***
## s(tumor_size)  1.000  1.000 10.074  0.00153 ** 
## s(bmi)         6.054  7.165  4.790 2.28e-05 ***
## s(Ki67)        1.000  1.000  5.286  0.02163 *  
## s(age)         1.000  1.000  5.189  0.02287 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.225   Deviance explained = 23.7%
## GCV = 0.47437  Scale est. = 0.46694   n = 1600

## [1] 0.7435168
## [1] 0.1642437

Generalization GAM on datB

## generalization on datB
# GAM trained on datA, predict on entire datB
gam_pred_datB <- predict(gam_fit, newdata = datB)

gam_rmse_datB <- sqrt(mean((datB$log_PFS - gam_pred_datB)^2))
gam_r2_datB <- cor(datB$log_PFS, gam_pred_datB)^2

gam_rmse_datB
## [1] 0.840068
gam_r2_datB
## [1] 0.154838

split

set.seed(123)
datsplit_b <- initial_split(datB, prop = 0.8)
train_b <- training(datsplit_b) |> select(-id)
test_b  <- testing(datsplit_b) |> select(-id)

re-build models on datB

# resampling setup
set.seed(123)
folds_b <- createMultiFolds(train_b$log_PFS, k = 10, times = 5)
ctrl_b <- trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 10,
  repeats = 5,
  index = folds_b
)

# LM
lm_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "lm", metric = "RMSE", trControl = ctrl_b)
# Ridge
ridge_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "glmnet", metric = "RMSE",
                     tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = 0, lambda = exp(seq(-5, 5, length = 50))),
                     trControl = ctrl_b)
# Lasso
lasso_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "glmnet", metric = "RMSE",
                     tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = 1, lambda = exp(seq(-5, 2, length = 50))),
                     trControl = ctrl_b)
## Warning in nominalTrainWorkflow(x = x, y = y, wts = weights, info = trainInfo,
## : There were missing values in resampled performance measures.
# Elastic Net
enet_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "glmnet", metric = "RMSE",
                    tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = seq(0, 1, length = 11),
                                           lambda = exp(seq(-5, 5, length = 50))),
                    trControl = ctrl_b)
## Warning in nominalTrainWorkflow(x = x, y = y, wts = weights, info = trainInfo,
## : There were missing values in resampled performance measures.
# GAM 
gam_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "gam", metric = "RMSE", trControl = ctrl_b)
# MARS
mars_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "earth", metric = "RMSE",
                    tuneGrid = expand.grid(degree = 1:3, nprune = 2:20),
                    trControl = ctrl_b)

# compare
resamp_b <- resamples(list(LM = lm_fit_b, Ridge = ridge_fit_b, Lasso = lasso_fit_b,
                           ENet = enet_fit_b, GAM = gam_fit_b, MARS = mars_fit_b))
summary(resamp_b)
## 
## Call:
## summary.resamples(object = resamp_b)
## 
## Models: LM, Ridge, Lasso, ENet, GAM, MARS 
## Number of resamples: 50 
## 
## MAE 
##            Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. NA's

## Ridge 0.4625812 0.5146315 0.5264451 0.5238728 0.5334051 0.5700600    0
## Lasso 0.4609747 0.5161153 0.5258329 0.5237806 0.5332668 0.5718899    0
## ENet  0.4625812 0.5146315 0.5264451 0.5238728 0.5334051 0.5700600    0
## GAM   0.4605715 0.5107385 0.5220598 0.5223793 0.5341737 0.5738752    0
## MARS  0.4624646 0.5104734 0.5214361 0.5228766 0.5362399 0.5789288    0
##

## Ridge 0.5792596 0.6392098 0.6550445 0.6555186 0.6727107 0.7129985    0
## Lasso 0.5784961 0.6403710 0.6552656 0.6560840 0.6730851 0.7172784    0
## ENet  0.5792596 0.6392098 0.6550445 0.6555186 0.6727107 0.7129985    0
## GAM   0.5761268 0.6392262 0.6489585 0.6531563 0.6701874 0.7216828    0
## MARS  0.5813993 0.6357279 0.6501705 0.6548886 0.6706589 0.7280406    0
## 
## Rsquared

## ENet  0.05870501 0.1580626 0.1883819 0.1843176 0.2147675 0.2890484    0
## GAM   0.06403179 0.1682200 0.1951541 0.1903121 0.2204090 0.2733632    0
## MARS  0.07395548 0.1652257 0.1871550 0.1853325 0.2153966 0.2575069    0
bwplot(resamp_b, metric = "RMSE")

GAM comparison across cohorts

# datA GAM summary and smooth curves
summary(gam_fit$finalModel)

plot(gam_fit_b$finalModel, pages = 1)

# datB test set evaluation
gam_pred_b <- predict(gam_fit_b, newdata = test_b)
gam_test_rmse_b <- sqrt(mean((test_b$log_PFS - gam_pred_b)^2))
gam_test_r2_b <- cor(test_b$log_PFS, gam_pred_b)^2
gam_test_rmse_b
## [1] 0.731265
gam_test_r2_b
## [1] 0.1406592

GAM before generalization RMSE: 0.840 after: 0.731265

Model selection based on the resampling results

If we want to choose LASSO as our final model:

# if we choose to use LASSO as the final model (because of easier interpretation)
lasso_pred <- predict(lasso_fit, newdata = test)
lasso_test_rmse <- sqrt(mean((test$log_PFS - lasso_pred)^2))
lasso_test_r2 <- cor(test$log_PFS, lasso_pred)^2

lasso_test_rmse
## [1] 0.7409103
lasso_test_r2
## [1] 0.1670126
# Lasso coefficients
coef(lasso_fit$finalModel, lasso_fit$bestTune$lambda)
## 20 x 1 sparse Matrix of class "dgCMatrix"
##                   s=0.01186285
## (Intercept)        2.657150940
## age               -0.002054392
## menopause1         .          
## race2              0.034665076
## race3              0.040004233
## race4             -0.024538538
## bmi                .          
## tumor_size        -0.056838168
## tumor_grade2      -0.002679517
## tumor_grade3      -0.096225273
## lymph_nodes       -0.050768833
## ER1                0.208477782
## PR1                .          
## HER21             -0.177361833
## Ki67              -0.005637774
## stage2             .          
## stage3             .          
## surgery1          -0.018630842
## chemo1             .          
## hormonal_therapy1  0.122289007

generalization on datB

# if we select LASSO
lasso_pred_datB <- predict(lasso_fit, newdata = datB)

lasso_rmse_datB <- sqrt(mean((datB$log_PFS - lasso_pred_datB)^2))
lasso_r2_datB <- cor(datB$log_PFS, lasso_pred_datB)^2

lasso_rmse_datB
## [1] 0.8295412
lasso_r2_datB
## [1] 0.1515466

split

set.seed(123)
datsplit_b <- initial_split(datB, prop = 0.8)
train_b <- training(datsplit_b) |> select(-id)
test_b  <- testing(datsplit_b) |> select(-id)

re-build models on datB

# resampling setup
set.seed(123)
folds_b <- createMultiFolds(train_b$log_PFS, k = 10, times = 5)
ctrl_b <- trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv",
  number = 10,
  repeats = 5,
  index = folds_b
)

# LM
lm_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "lm", metric = "RMSE", trControl = ctrl_b)
# Ridge
ridge_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "glmnet", metric = "RMSE",
                     tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = 0, lambda = exp(seq(-5, 5, length = 50))),
                     trControl = ctrl_b)
# Lasso
lasso_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "glmnet", metric = "RMSE",
                     tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = 1, lambda = exp(seq(-5, 2, length = 50))),
                     trControl = ctrl_b)
## Warning in nominalTrainWorkflow(x = x, y = y, wts = weights, info = trainInfo,
## : There were missing values in resampled performance measures.
# Elastic Net
enet_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "glmnet", metric = "RMSE",
                    tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = seq(0, 1, length = 11),
                                           lambda = exp(seq(-5, 5, length = 50))),
                    trControl = ctrl_b)
## Warning in nominalTrainWorkflow(x = x, y = y, wts = weights, info = trainInfo,
## : There were missing values in resampled performance measures.
# GAM 
gam_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "gam", metric = "RMSE", trControl = ctrl_b)
# MARS
mars_fit_b <- train(log_PFS ~ ., data = train_b, method = "earth", metric = "RMSE",
                    tuneGrid = expand.grid(degree = 1:3, nprune = 2:20),
                    trControl = ctrl_b)

# compare
resamp_b <- resamples(list(LM = lm_fit_b, Ridge = ridge_fit_b, Lasso = lasso_fit_b,
                           ENet = enet_fit_b, GAM = gam_fit_b, MARS = mars_fit_b))
summary(resamp_b)
## 
## Call:
## summary.resamples(object = resamp_b)
## 
## Models: LM, Ridge, Lasso, ENet, GAM, MARS 
## Number of resamples: 50 
## 
## MAE 
##            Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. NA's
## LM    0.4577066 0.5153166 0.5254589 0.5247340 0.5354314 0.5767516    0
## Ridge 0.4625812 0.5146315 0.5264451 0.5238728 0.5334051 0.5700600    0
## Lasso 0.4609747 0.5161153 0.5258329 0.5237806 0.5332668 0.5718899    0
## ENet  0.4625812 0.5146315 0.5264451 0.5238728 0.5334051 0.5700600    0
## GAM   0.4605715 0.5107385 0.5220598 0.5223793 0.5341737 0.5738752    0
## MARS  0.4624646 0.5104734 0.5214361 0.5228766 0.5362399 0.5789288    0
## 
## RMSE 
##            Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. NA's
## LM    0.5771560 0.6410196 0.6553600 0.6570379 0.6719280 0.7219398    0
## Ridge 0.5792596 0.6392098 0.6550445 0.6555186 0.6727107 0.7129985    0
## Lasso 0.5784961 0.6403710 0.6552656 0.6560840 0.6730851 0.7172784    0
## ENet  0.5792596 0.6392098 0.6550445 0.6555186 0.6727107 0.7129985    0
## GAM   0.5761268 0.6392262 0.6489585 0.6531563 0.6701874 0.7216828    0
## MARS  0.5813993 0.6357279 0.6501705 0.6548886 0.6706589 0.7280406    0
## 
## Rsquared 
##             Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. NA's
## LM    0.06062592 0.1490517 0.1883065 0.1806123 0.2108733 0.2805334    0
## Ridge 0.05870501 0.1580626 0.1883819 0.1843176 0.2147675 0.2890484    0
## Lasso 0.06050890 0.1524987 0.1918682 0.1826220 0.2104570 0.2903568    0
## ENet  0.05870501 0.1580626 0.1883819 0.1843176 0.2147675 0.2890484    0
## GAM   0.06403179 0.1682200 0.1951541 0.1903121 0.2204090 0.2733632    0
## MARS  0.07395548 0.1652257 0.1871550 0.1853325 0.2153966 0.2575069    0
bwplot(resamp_b, metric = "RMSE")

coefficient of LASSO

coefficient of GAM

# automatically non-linear variables by caret
# datA
summary(gam_fit$finalModel)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## .outcome ~ menopause1 + race2 + race3 + race4 + tumor_grade2 + 
##     tumor_grade3 + ER1 + PR1 + HER21 + stage2 + stage3 + surgery1 + 
##     chemo1 + hormonal_therapy1 + s(lymph_nodes) + s(tumor_size) + 
##     s(bmi) + s(Ki67) + s(age)
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)        2.064124   0.085336  24.188  < 2e-16 ***
## menopause1         0.060813   0.051741   1.175  0.24004    
## race2              0.078155   0.049164   1.590  0.11210    
## race3              0.092519   0.062178   1.488  0.13696    
## race4             -0.042246   0.052874  -0.799  0.42441    
## tumor_grade2      -0.072889   0.051044  -1.428  0.15350    
## tumor_grade3      -0.176677   0.065561  -2.695  0.00712 ** 
## ER1                0.242558   0.057105   4.248 2.29e-05 ***
## PR1               -0.028336   0.042651  -0.664  0.50655    
## HER21             -0.205080   0.042287  -4.850 1.36e-06 ***
## stage2             0.036962   0.057158   0.647  0.51795    
## stage3             0.090455   0.112130   0.807  0.41996    
## surgery1          -0.039580   0.036655  -1.080  0.28040    
## chemo1             0.004067   0.035795   0.114  0.90955    
## hormonal_therapy1  0.128418   0.043718   2.937  0.00336 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                  edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## s(lymph_nodes) 1.000  1.000 27.684 4.74e-07 ***
## s(tumor_size)  1.000  1.000 10.074  0.00153 ** 
## s(bmi)         6.054  7.165  4.790 2.28e-05 ***
## s(Ki67)        1.000  1.000  5.286  0.02163 *  
## s(age)         1.000  1.000  5.189  0.02287 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.225   Deviance explained = 23.7%
## GCV = 0.47437  Scale est. = 0.46694   n = 1600
plot(gam_fit$finalModel)

#datB
summary(gam_fit_b$finalModel)

plot(gam_fit_b$finalModel)

懒得打英语了hhh,可以对比一下lasso和gam,共同点: 1. HER2效应消失 2. bmi的非线性 我觉得重点我们可以解释模型的前后coefficient的变化
